summary:
• Infrastructure & Accessibility: The largest ADA playground in Chattanooga is set to open soon, enhancing outdoor access and inclusivity.
  • Education & Workforce Development: Hamilton County Schools receive a $9 million investment to expand skilled trade career access, strengthening future workforce capabilities.
  • Public Safety Enhancement: 26 new firefighters have graduated from the Chattanooga Fire Department's academy, bolstering the city's emergency response capacity.

items.content_text:
One of our core commitments is “access to the outdoors”. This is a major step. Soon the largest ADA playground in the city will open. And we are not done.
The newly paved section of the Cherokee Trail at Stringer's Ridge is officially open! Thanks to Joel and Abigail, as well as District 2 Councilwoman Jenny Hill and our One Chattanooga team who helped bring this project to fruition. This is just phase one of the three-phase plan, and we are excited to share progress updates with you as we have them. Located at the High Ridge trailhead at
 898 High Ridge Drive. 
 This project is funded under a grant contract with the state of Tennessee.

items.title: 🚨 LAKESIDE LIVE UPDATE: Rubiks Groove Postponed to Friday, July 24 🚨 🌧️ Mother Nature had other plans for Saturday, J...
items.content_text:
🚨 LAKESIDE LIVE UPDATE: Rubiks Groove Postponed to Friday, July 24 🚨
 🌧️ Mother Nature had other plans for Saturday, July 11... but she's not canceling the party—just hitting pause! 🎸🎉
 To make sure everyone gets the full Rubiks Groove experience, we've postponed the concert to Friday, July 24. The band was just as excited to play for the Chester Frost crowd as we were, and they're coming back for the rain date to make it an unforgettable night!
 🎶 Music starts at 7:00 PM
 🛥️ New for the July 24 show: Boating in? Pull up to Chester Frost, tune your radio to 87.9 FM, and enjoy the concert live from the water!
 ☀️ We know forecasts don't always get it exactly right, but decisions like this have to be made ahead of time. We'd much rather give everyone a great concert than gamble with the weather. Thanks for rolling with us!
 Mark your calendars, tell your crew, pack your lawn chairs, and we'll see you Lakeside on Friday, July 24! 🌅🎶
 #LakesideLive #ChesterFrostPark #RubiksGroove #HamiltonCountyParks

items.title: 🚨 Chattanooga’s Newest Firefighters! 🚨  A class of 26 cadets made it through our rigorous six-month fire academy to be...
items.content_text:
🚨 Chattanooga’s Newest Firefighters! 🚨 
 A class of 26 cadets made it through our rigorous six-month fire academy to become the newest members of the Chattanooga Fire Department! They successfully completed Emergency Medical Technician training and learned about all of the different aspects of the fire service from other highly skilled, veteran firefighters. Our Training Division and other CFD personnel taught them about fire department operations, fire attack, fire growth & development, fire suppression, and search & rescue techniques. They’ve also mastered a number of skills like hazardous materials response, vehicle extrication, rope rescues, ventilation, and survival tactics- whether they have to save a fellow downed firefighter or save themselves from a fire should they ever become trapped, disoriented, or separated from their crew.
 This class is ready to serve the citizens of Chattanooga and they will immediately head out into our fire companies to work at our 20 fire stations. These firefighters are the future of our department and we wish them all the best as they embark on their careers. 
 Thanks to Bayside Baptist Church for allowing us to hold graduation there!

items.title: Williams Island is 450 acres and just a few miles down river from downtown Chattanooga. Accessible by boat, you can camp...
items.content_text:
Williams Island is 450 acres and just a few miles down river from downtown Chattanooga.
 Accessible by boat, you can camp on the island as you explore the Tennessee River Gorge. 🛶
 Trivia time:
 🐄 The island has been inhabited for over 12k years and used for agricultural use—it still is today with a working cow farm!
 🐦 This island serves as an important stop for many migratory birds.
 🏕️ single and group camp sites are available for free! You just have to reserve them on Tennessee River Gorge Trust ‘s website.
 #paddle #tennessee #camping #visitchatt #nationalparkcity

items.title: What a great evening celebrating another successful year with the East Ridge Council of the Chattanooga Chamber of Comme...
items.content_text:
What a great evening celebrating another successful year with the East Ridge Council of the Chattanooga Chamber of Commerce in #EastRidgeTN! 🎉
 Thank you to everyone who attended, the sponsors who made the event possible, and Topgolf Chattanooga for hosting such a fun evening. We’re grateful for the Chamber’s continued partnership and commitment to supporting East Ridge’s business community.
 Congratulations to Amy Silvers and Shawnna Skiles on their years of dedicated service to the East Ridge Council Board, and best wishes to the incoming leadership as they continue building on that momentum! 💙 #EastRidgeTN
Last night's end of year celebration was a blast!  Thank you to our host, Topgolf, for providing such a wonderful venue, great food, and lots of fun for the attendees.  We'd also like to thank our sponsors for helping making this event possible:
 ▪️McAllister's Deli
 ▪️The Brothers That Just Do Gutters 
 ▪️FirstBank
 ▪️Lumberjacks Tree Service and Hardwood Center 
 ▪️Brookdale Mount Vernon 
 ▪️ASA Engineering and Consulting 
 ▪️Bailey Manor
 ▪️Time Finder
 ▪️Smoothie King
 ▪️The Georgekutty Family 
 ▪️Pro Logo Depot
 We'd also like to recognize two Board members in particular, as their terms have come to an end.  First our President, Amy Silvers, for her volunteer leadership and passion for connecting businesses and people over the past two years. Next, Shawnna Skiles for five years of dedication and a real "get it done" work ethic that has been invaluable to our mission. You both will be missed!  Congratulations to our Board of Directors and everyone that contributed to another successful year!  We appreciate you. 
 If anyone has pictures of the event to share, please post them in the comments.

items.title: As Chattanooga 2.0 continues to grow and deepen our impact across Hamilton County, we're excited to announce an expansion of Jenni...
items.content_text:
As Chattanooga 2.0 continues to grow and deepen our impact across Hamilton County, we're excited to announce an expansion of Jennifer Andrews' leadership role. Jennifer has been promoted to Chief Operating Officer, where she will help strengthen the organization's operational capacity while continuing to lead Chattanooga 2.0's countywide early childhood strategy.

Jennifer joined Chattanooga 2.0 nine years ago and is our longest-tenured employee. During that time, she has helped build many of the collaborative efforts that have shaped Hamilton County's early childhood landscape, including the growth of Early Matters Chattanooga, the Quality Matters Fund, the Ready, Set, Kindergarten campaign, and neighborhood-based initiatives that support children and families.

In her expanded role, Jennifer will help strengthen Chattanooga 2.0's internal operations and organizational infrastructure while continuing to serve as our lead for early childhood strategy and the primary point of contact for Early Matters and our countywide early childhood partnerships. 

Please join us in congratulating Jennifer on this exciting next chapter!

items.title: Now Hiring in #EastRidgeTN! 🚔 The City of East Ridge is currently accepting applications for the position of Police Off...
items.content_text:
Now Hiring in #EastRidgeTN! 🚔
 The City of East Ridge is currently accepting applications for the position of Police Officer with the East Ridge Police Department.
 Join a department committed to professionalism, integrity, and community service while building a rewarding career protecting and serving the citizens of East Ridge. 
 Qualifications:
 ✔️ Tennessee P.O.S.T. Certification
 ✔️ High School Diploma or GED
 ✔️ Must successfully complete physical, psychological, drug screening, background, written, and oral examinations
 What We Offer:
 👏🏼 Starting salary of $59,454.81 annually
 👏🏼 $10,000 Recruitment & Retention Bonus for P.O.S.T. Certified Officers
 👏🏼 $8,000 Recruitment & Retention Bonus for Entry-Level Officers (No Previous Experience)
 👏🏼 Take-home vehicle, competitive benefits, and retirement pension
 👏🏼 12-hour rotating shifts with every other weekend off
 👏🏼 Specialized unit opportunities including K-9, SWAT, Traffic, CSU, and School Resource Officer
 Join a team dedicated to protecting our community while making a lasting impact in the lives of those who call #EastRidgeTN home.
 A City of East Ridge application is required to be considered for an open position. 
 📧 Please email your resume and cover letter to msinigaglio@eastridgetn.gov. 
 Note that if an application has not been submitted within the last 60 days, please reapply. 
 💻 Apply online today at:  www.eastridgetn.gov/human_resources.html
 The City of East Ridge is an Equal Opportunity Employer and a Tennessee Drug-Free Workplace.

items.title: Today we’re celebrating a major investment in the future of Hamilton County students! HCS has been selected as one of ju...
items.content_text:
Today we’re celebrating a major investment in the future of Hamilton County students!
 
HCS has been selected as one of just nine regions nationwide, and one of only two locations in the Southeast, to participate in Bloomberg Philanthropies’ national skilled trades initiative.

With $9 million in support from Bloomberg Philanthropies, HCS Future Ready Centers and the Construction Career Center will be enhanced through facility upgrades, new equipment, expanded staffing and programming, and a competency-based portfolio system that validates students' skills and readiness for employers and unions.

This milestone is the result of an incredible partnership between Hamilton County Schools, Chattanooga 2.0, Hamilton County Government, the City of Chattanooga, AGC East Tennessee, IBEW Local 175, and UA Local 43 Plumbers & Steamfitters. We are so thankful for our partners and their commitment to building brighter futures for our students.

Read more here: https://bit.ly/4voIirJ.

items.title: 🌿 Make Your Yard RainSmart & Reduce Your Stormwater Fees! 💧 The City of Red Bank is proud to offer the RainSmart Yards...
items.content_text:
🌿 Make Your Yard RainSmart & Reduce Your Stormwater Fees! 💧
 The City of Red Bank is proud to offer the RainSmart Yards program — a simple way for homeowners to help reduce stormwater runoff and protect our local creeks and streams. By managing the rainwater on your property with practices like rain gardens, native plantings, and rain barrels, you can:
 ✅ Improve local water quality
 ✅ Reduce pollution flowing into Red Bank's stormwater system and waterways
 ✅ Create habitat for beneficial pollinators
 ✅ Help our community stay flood-resilient
 And here’s the great news:
 If your yard earns RainSmart certification, you can receive a stormwater utility fee discount from the City of Red Bank based on your rating:
 🌟 Bronze – 25% stormwater fee reduction

items.content_html:
🌿 Make Your Yard RainSmart & Reduce Your Stormwater Fees! 💧
The City of Red Bank is proud to offer the RainSmart Yards program — a simple way for homeowners to help reduce stormwater runoff and protect our local creeks and streams. By managing the rainwater on your property with practices like rain gardens, native plantings, and rain barrels, you can:
✅ Improve local water quality
✅ Reduce pollution flowing into Red Bank's stormwater system and waterways
✅ Create habitat for beneficial pollinators
✅ Help our community stay flood-resilient
And here’s the great news:
If your yard earns RainSmart certification, you can receive a stormwater utility fee discount from the City of Red Bank based on your rating:
🌟 Bronze – 25% stormwater fee reduction
🌟 Silver – 50% stormwater fee reduction
🏆 A Certified yard = lower annual stormwater fee!
Participation also gives you access to free workshops and resources to help you get started.
Learn more and take the first step toward certification: mywaterways.org/rainsmart-yards
Let’s work together to keep Red Bank’s waterways healthy — and save money while we do it! 🌎💙
#CleanWater #RainSmart #Stormwater

items.title: CPD Investigating Woman's Death After Body Found At Lookout Valley Hotel CHATTANOOGA, Tenn. -- The Chattanooga Police De...
items.content_text:
CPD Investigating Woman's Death After Body Found At Lookout Valley Hotel
 CHATTANOOGA, Tenn. -- The Chattanooga Police Department (CPD) is investigating the death of a woman whose remains were found at a Lookout Valley hotel Wednesday evening.
 At approximately 4:25 p.m., officers responded to the 100 block of Patten Chapel Road. Upon arrival, they discovered a deceased woman in the parking lot. CPD's Crime Scene and Homicide Units responded to process and investigate the incident. 
 Investigators identified the victim as 22-year-old Elysia Kinsella, who was reported missing last week. Her cause of death remains under investigation.
 Donte Taylor (DOB: 04/16/1986) was arrested and charged with Abuse of a Corpse and Tampering with Evidence after admitting to leaving the victim's body in the parking lot. Taylor is in custody at the Hamilton County Jail and Detention Center.
 This information is preliminary and subject to change as the investigation continues. Chattanooga Police ask anyone with information regarding this incident to call 423-698-2525.
 The charges and allegations referenced in this release are merely accusations of criminal conduct and not evidence. A defendant is presumed innocent unless and until proven guilty beyond a reasonable doubt and convicted through due process of law.
 #CPDnews

Hamilton County and Chattanooga are helping build the workforce of tomorrow.
 We’re proud to partner with @BloombergDotOrg on a new initiative connecting more young people in our community to high-wage, in-demand skilled trades careers.
 By bringing together schools, labor unions, employers, and local leaders, this effort will expand student access to hands-on technical training, paid work-based learning, and pathways into Registered Apprenticeships and strengthen the workforce our economy depends on.
 In collaboration with Hamilton County Schools, the City of Chattanooga, labor unions, employers, and community partners, we’re creating more pathways to sustaining careers while strengthening the workforce our local economy depends on.

Another great Tennessee Chapter, NECA Chattanooga Division Fish Fry yesterday!
 A huge thank you to everyone who joined us for our Annual NECA Fish Fry, hosted by the Chattanooga JATC at their outstanding training lab facility. Chattanooga Electrical Apprenticeship and Training Center
 With more than 80 attendees representing our NECA contractor members, it was a great afternoon of fellowship, networking, and strengthening relationships across our industry. 
 We were especially honored to welcome Congressman Chuck Fleischmann, along with members of his staff, and Hamilton County Mayor Weston Wamp and his staff. We sincerely appreciate them taking time out of their busy schedules to visit with our members and support the electrical construction industry. 
 As always, Uncle Larry’s Restaurant served an incredible fish dinner that everyone enjoyed!
 Thank you to the Chattanooga Electrical Apprenticeship for hosting, to Uncle Larry’s for another fantastic meal, and to everyone who attended and helped make this year’s Fish Fry such a success. We look forward to seeing everyone again next year!
 Hamilton County Mayor’s Office
 Uncle Larry's Restaurant
 National Electrical Contractors Association - NECA

items.title: As siting of megaprojects accelerates globally, what lessons from nuclear energy projects offer insights regarding the value of pu...
items.content_text:
As siting of megaprojects accelerates globally, what lessons from nuclear energy projects offer insights regarding the value of public engagement? Kelle Barfield, Class of 2024-26, explored answers to this question as part of the Executive MSc in Cities at LSE.
Nuclear Megaprojects

The Executive MSc in Cities is an 18-month programme for urban professionals, combining academic insight with real-world impact. A cornerstone of the programme is the Urban Consultancy Project - a live, client-facing engagement where participants tackle a pressing urban challenge in partnership with their own or a host organisation.
 
For her consultancy, Kelle researched examples across the life cycle of nuclear energy production - a nuclear new build in Canada, nuclear plant restarts in Japan, and construction of a spent fuel repository in Switzerland - to examine similar approaches to public engagement supporting successful nuclear project deployment.

Learn more here: https://lnkd.in/ek2ZrxWy 

#LSECities #ExecutiveMScInCities

items.title: Remembering former ULGC President/CEO Jerome W. Page It is with deep sadness that we announce the passing of Jerome Whit...
items.content_text:
Remembering former ULGC President/CEO Jerome W. Page
 It is with deep sadness that we announce the passing of Jerome Whittington Page, the former President and CEO of the Urban League of Greater Chattanooga, who left us on February 8, 2026, at the age of 90. Jerome served as a transformative leader of the organization from 1983 to 1991, leaving an indelible mark on the community and the Urban League’s legacy.
 Born on March 6, 1935, in Denver, Colorado, Jerome devoted his life to advocating for civil rights, social justice, and economic opportunity. Under his leadership, the Urban League became a beacon of hope and progress, championing youth development and workforce readiness at a pivotal time in history.
 Jerome was a visionary who launched numerous initiatives, including the Equal Opportunity Day Dinner and the Youth Achievement and Motivation Project, which helped prepare young people for futures filled with potential. His unwavering belief in Black excellence and commitment to service paved the way for countless individuals in the Chattanooga community to succeed.
 Jerome Page was surrounded by loved ones in his final moments, and he is survived by his devoted wife, JoKatherine Holliman Page; his sons; his grandchildren; and an extended family who cherished him deeply.
 A Homegoing Service to honor Jerome's life will be held on Saturday, July 25th, at 11:00 AM at Holy Redeemer Episcopal Church, located at 2552 North Williams St, Denver, CO 80205. 
 As we reflect on his life and contributions, we celebrate a legacy of service and dedication to inclusion, justice, and community empowerment. In honor of Jerome Page, let us continue to champion the values he lived by and strive to uplift our community, ensuring that his spirit of service and advocacy lives on.
 Rest in peace, former President Page. Your legacy will forever inspire us.

 The Hamilton County Health Department is dedicated to providing secure and timely access to your protected health information (PHI).
 You can submit requests for medical records through a Release of Information (ROI) request. Immunization records can be obtained by phone or email.

Good morning, community. In recent days we have received calls and confirmed recent activity from immigration authorities...
items.content_text:
Good morning community
 In recent days we have received calls and confirmed recent activity from immigration authorities in some of our communities. We want to encourage you to stay informed, act calmly and always prioritize your safety.
 If you know that there is activity near your neighborhood or in a place you frequent, and it is not necessary to leave home, we recommend staying in a safe place and avoiding unnecessary trips. If you do not have a valid driver's license, avoid driving.
 We also remind you that all people have rights, regardless of their immigration status. If you are detained or if officers arrive at your door, it is important to know those rights and how to respond.
 For that reason, we share two videos in Spanish with information on what to do if you find yourself in any of these situations. Our goal is for you and your families to be prepared and informed, not to act out of fear.
 WE HAVE RIGHTS: In Our Communities, On Our Streets (Spanish)
 https://youtu.be/jNBBJs0ZLs0?si=yduVMGiq5vvyIJT2
 WE HAVE RIGHTS: Inside Our Homes (Spanish)
 https://youtu.be/qZhKv93wOfs?si=IVsThJfR1tTCwHRD
 If you have not already done so, we recommend talking to your family about an emergency plan, having important documents organized, and having contact information for a trusted attorney.
 As always, we invite you to stay informed through reliable sources and to take care of yourself and your loved ones. If you have any questions please call La Paz at (423) 624-8414.  
 @followers

IBMA Member Presale: July 14 at 10 AM CT

items.title: Your voice matters. Your vote matters. Your creativity matters. Join us for Beats & Ballots, an interactive evening wher...
items.content_text:
Your voice matters. Your vote matters. Your creativity matters.
 Join us for Beats & Ballots, an interactive evening where music, community, and civic engagement come together.
 The first half of the evening is all about connection and learning. Meet other young professionals, enjoy great music, and hear why local elections have a direct impact on our neighborhoods, schools, businesses, and future.
 Then we’ll roll up our sleeves.
 In the second half, we’ll work together to create materials that encourage our community to get out and vote in the upcoming local election on August 6. 
 Friday, July 10
 6:00–8:30 PM
 Therapy 423 Lounge
 1518 Market St.
 Chattanooga, TN
 Let’s create something that inspires Chattanooga to show up on August 6. Invite a friend and be part of the movement.
 Register Here: https://fundraise.givesmart.com/form/2PRi1A?vid=1ra5t0

Another quick stop made this morning by Red Shift! At 5:32 a.m., companies responded to a report of a living room fire a...
items.content_text:
Another quick stop made this morning by Red Shift! At 5:32 a.m., companies responded to a report of a living room fire at Mill Town complex (2300 block of E 18th St Pl). Upon arrival, crews observed heavy smoke coming from a first floor unit, on the front and side of the building. With assistance from CPD, occupants began evacuating from the building. In less than 15 minutes from the time of dispatch, firefighters were able to knock down the fire resulting in no injuries to occupants or crews. Thanks to the quick response, the incident was contained to a single unit, preventing further damage to the building. The cause is under investigation at this time.
 Responding companies: 
 Squad 13, Engine 4, Ladder 1, Engine 1, Squad 1, Engine 5, Ladder 5

At 8:42 p.m. this evening, Chattanooga Fire Department Red Shift companies were dispatched to a single-story residential fire on the 4200 block of Dodds Ave. Firefighters were able to get the flames under control quickly, and the fire was contained to the room of origin. 
 There were no injuries. One resident and their pet have been displaced. The cause is under investigation at this time.
 Responding companies:
 Squad 1, Squad 20, Engine 5, Ladder 5, Engine 14, Engine 9

items.title: Ever notice those outlets with the little "Test" and "Reset" buttons? They're called GFCI outlets, and they're designed...
items.content_text:
Ever notice those outlets with the little "Test" and "Reset" buttons? They're called GFCI outlets, and they're designed to help protect you from electrical shock in areas where water and electricity could meet, like kitchens, bathrooms, garages, and outdoors.
 By shutting off power in a fraction of a second when they detect a fault, GFCI outlets add an important layer of protection for your home and family.
 Watch to learn how they work and why they matter. ⚡🏠

🌱 From Seed to Success! 🌻🍅
 What a wonderful example of how a small seed can grow into something amazing! One of our Head Start parents participated in our Seed Giveaway, planted their seeds at home, and look at the incredible results! 🌿
 This beautiful garden is more than just fresh vegetables and flowers—it represents family time, hands-on learning, healthy eating, patience, and the joy of watching something grow together.
 We love seeing our families take what they learn through our program and bring it home. Thank you for sharing your gardening journey and inspiring other Head Start families to plant, grow, and thrive! 💚
 📸 Check out the amazing results from this family's garden!

items.content_text:
Help Shape the Future of Southeast Tennessee! 

📍 We're Hiring: Regional Planner

Are you passionate about helping communities grow and thrive? Southeast Tennessee Development District is seeking a Regional Planner to join our Planning Department and work alongside local governments across Southeast Tennessee on projects that shape the future of our region.

From comprehensive planning and land use to transportation, grant development, and community development initiatives, this position offers the opportunity to make a meaningful impact while working with communities throughout our 10-county region.

If you're looking for a career where planning meets purpose, we'd love to hear from you.

View the full job description in the attached PDF. Applications will be accepted until the position is filled.

Help Shape the Future of Southeast Tennessee! 

📍 We're Hiring: Regional Planner

Are you passionate about helping communities grow and thrive? Southeast Tennessee Development District is seeking a Regional Planner to join our Planning Department and work alongside local governments across Southeast Tennessee on projects that shape the future of our region.

From comprehensive planning and land use to transportation, grant development, and community development initiatives, this position offers the opportunity to make a meaningful impact while working with communities throughout our 10-county region.

If you're looking for a career where planning meets purpose, we'd love to hear from you.

View the full job description in the attached PDF. Applications will be accepted until the position is filled.

Thomas Gass, currently Ambassador of Switzerland and Head of Mission in Hanoi, Viet Nam, was appointed today as the new Chair of the #OECD’s Development Assistance Committee (#DAC). 
 The DAC is a forum for its 34 members and Associates to agree on international principles, rules and standards for development co-operation.   
 The DAC also publishes regular data and analysis on Official Development Assistance (ODA) flows and monitors how members deliver on their commitments through Peer Reviews.

items.title: CPD Investigating East Brainerd Rd Shooting CHATTANOOGA, Tenn. -- The Chattanooga Police Department (CPD) is investigati...
items.content_text:
CPD Investigating East Brainerd Rd Shooting
 CHATTANOOGA, Tenn. -- The Chattanooga Police Department (CPD) is investigating a shooting that happened in East Brainerd Tuesday evening.
 At approximately 6:10 p.m., officers responded to a reported shooting in the 7700 block of East Brainerd Road . When officers arrived on scene, they found a 19-year-old man with non-life-threatening injuries. Hamilton County EMS transported the victim to the hospital for treatment.
 CPD's Crime Scene and Homicide Units responded to process the scene and investigate the incident. The preliminary investigation indicates the 19-year-old and a juvenile were driving down East Brainerd Road when someone shot at them from another vehicle. The juvenile was not injured. 
 This remains an active and ongoing investigation. This information is preliminary and subject to change as the investigation continues. Chattanooga Police ask anyone with information regarding this incident to call 423-698-2525.
 #CPDnews

This year marks their 40th anniversary, and we want to celebrate the people who have made Komatsu what it is today. Here's to 40 years of innovation, opportunity and the people who made it all possible. 💙 

#BuiltByChattanooga

items.title: ArtsBuild’s Periscope CHA 2027 Applications Now Open through August 7 Artist entrepreneurs are invited to apply for the...
items.content_text:
ArtsBuild’s Periscope CHA 2027 Applications Now Open through August 7
 Artist entrepreneurs are invited to apply for the 2027 class
 CHATTANOOGA, TN – ArtsBuild is accepting applications for its 2027 Periscope CHA: Artist Entrepreneur Training program now through August 7, 2026. All working artists looking to transform their creative passions into thriving businesses are encouraged to apply. There is no application fee, and if accepted, the program participation fee is on a sliding scale of $50 - $500 based on income, and payment plans are available. 
 Periscope, originally created by the Arts & Business Council of Greater Nashville, has been empowering artists for over a decade. Launched in 2014, arrived in Chattanooga in 2023. It features a 10-week intensive curriculum followed by six months of personalized mentorship, culminating in the celebrated Pitch Night and Entrepreneur Expo, sponsored by Tennessee Valley Federal Credit Union. The pitch event is a vibrant celebration where Periscope artists showcase their entrepreneurial skills for the chance to take home cash prizes up to $10,000.
 Artists, artisans, musicians, performers, makers, and creative entrepreneurs of all artistic disciplines in the Greater Chattanooga region are invited to apply for the Periscope CHA Class of 2027. ArtsBuild is also offering two Informational Sessions for potential applicants on Tuesday, July 21, 2026, with a virtual session at 10:30 am and an in-person session at 5:30 pm. RSVP to one of the sessions at artsbuild.com/periscope.
 ArtsBuild is deeply grateful for the following sponsors and supporters of Periscope CHA: Benwood Foundation, City of Chattanooga, The Howard Fund at the Community Foundation of Greater Chattanooga, The Nancy Lackey Community Education Fund, RISE Chattanooga, The Sankofa Fund for Civic Engagement, Tennessee Department of Economic Development, Tennessee Valley Federal Credit Union, and United Way of Greater Chattanooga.

items.title: Here's the latest on our lovely bridge 🎉 This fall can't come soon enough!I got to spend my morning taking a look at al...
items.content_text:
Here's the latest on our lovely bridge 🎉 This fall can't come soon enough!
I got to spend my morning taking a look at all of the progress being made on the beloved Walnut Street Bridge!
 Painting is complete, and brand-new decking stretches across the entire span of the bridge. Crews are now wrapping up final electrical work (including new lighting and cameras) and have begun removing construction platforms. Very soon, benches, flower pots, nameplates, and historic signs, will be back in their original spots. We are incredibly grateful for the patience and love this community has shown. The bridge is on track for a late September completion, with a grand celebration to follow — stay tuned!

items.title: Yesterday, HCSO personnel spent time with students and participated in the 2026 Team Mentoring, Inc.’s Girls' Golf Exper...
items.content_text:
Yesterday, HCSO personnel spent time with students and participated in the 2026 Team Mentoring, Inc.’s Girls' Golf Experience at the Bear Trace Golf Course in Harrison Bay, in partnership with LPGA*USGA Girls Golf.
 This event is one of many hosted by Team Mentoring, Inc. each year, giving youth opportunities to try new things through recreational and educational experiences, connect with professionals and community leaders, meet women serving in leadership roles, and have positive interactions with members of law enforcement.
 “Partnerships with youth organizations like Team Mentoring, Inc. play a vital role in building meaningful, lasting relationships between the HCSO and our county’s youth. Now more than ever, it’s essential for law enforcement to work with and support groups that provide positive, educational opportunities for our young people and help develop the next generation of leaders in our county,” stated Sheriff Austin Garrett.
 A special thank you to all the HCSO personnel who spent time at today’s event, as well as our partners at Team Mentoring, Inc., and their Executive Director, Freddie L. Brooks, a retired Chattanooga Fire Department firefighter and friend of Sheriff Garrett.

items.title: Childhood development does not start in preschool or kindergarten; it begins at birth, with each stage laying the founda...
items.content_text:
Childhood development does not start in preschool or kindergarten; it begins at birth, with each stage laying the foundation for the next.
 From birth to 12 months, babies form early connections, communicate, and develop attachments through interaction and care.
 From ages 1 to 3 years, children become explorers, enhancing their language skills, movement, and early emotional awareness.
 At ages 4 to 5 years, children further strengthen their communication, social skills, problem-solving abilities, and early learning through play and routine.
 Development is a continuous process that encompasses language, motor skills, cognitive abilities, and social-emotional growth.
 Parents as Teachers supports families through every stage of early development from birth through kindergarten.
 Are you interested in the Parents as Teachers program? 
 For more information, please call (423) 209-8298 or visit [Parents as Teachers](https://health.hamiltontn.org/…/childservices/parentsasteac…).

items.title: Alongside 23 educators representing 16 public and private schools across Chattanooga and Hamilton County, Carter Graham...
items.content_text:
Alongside 23 educators representing 16 public and private schools across Chattanooga and Hamilton County, Carter Graham heard from industry and research leaders, including Dr. Rick Mukherjee, director of the UTC Quantum Center, and Paul Smith, manager of quantum computing at EPB, about the growing impact of quantum research and technology.
 Mukherjee emphasized the importance of quantum education at the middle and high school levels, as quantum will impact industries such as healthcare, logistics and financial services.
 “We have professors from different departments associated with the Quantum Center, including mathematics, electrical engineering, computer science and physics,” he said. “Some of them are involved in teaching aspects of quantum. Others are involved on the research side, but we have a truly interdisciplinary program.”
 🔗  Read more here: https://blog.utc.edu/…/bringing-quantum-to-the-classroom-t…/

items.title: “It’s been a great career!” Congratulations to Captain Tony Manis on his retirement from the Chattanooga Fire Department...
items.content_text:
“It’s been a great career!” Congratulations to Captain Tony Manis on his retirement from the Chattanooga Fire Department after 30 years!
 Tony carried on a rich legacy of service in the CFD, following his father and brother who were also firefighters. He earned the respect of his crew—not only for his knowledge and work ethic, but for his willingness to teach, mentor, and invest in the next generation. His coworkers enjoyed working with him and learning from him. 
 His passion for the fire service was evident in everything he did and he will be greatly missed. We wish him the very best in his well-earned retirement. Thank you for your many years of dedication to this department and to the community you faithfully served!

items.title: In this episode, which is part of our special series Curating Tennessee: 1776-2076, we're joined by Liza Blair, a curato...
items.content_text:
In this episode, which is part of our special series Curating Tennessee: 1776-2076, we're joined by Liza Blair, a curator and museum specialist at Chattanooga State Community College and the curator of Humanities Tennessee's upcoming exhibit What If: Designs from the Future of Crafting and Growing. The exhibit opens August 8, 2026, at the Museum and Cultural Center at 5ive Points in Cleveland, Tennessee. Listen to the episode by searching for Humanities in Action wherever you get your podcasts or click here: https://www.iheart.com/…/curating-tennessee-with-liza-blair…
 --
 This episode is part of By the People: Conversations Beyond 250, a series of community-driven programs created by humanities councils in collaboration with local partners. The initiative was developed by the Federation of State Humanities Councils  and Smithsonian Folklife.
 This project is also funded under an agreement with the State of Tennessee administered by the Tennessee Commission for the United States Semiquincentennial (Tennessee 250).

🗳️ Your voice matters, but you can only make your voice heard if you're registered to vote!

The deadline to register for the August election is today, July 7.

Whether you're registering for the first time, updating your information after a move, or checking your voter registration, taking a few minutes today ensures you're ready to make your voice heard.

Your vote helps shape decisions that impact our schools, roads, public safety, healthcare, and the future of Hamilton County.

items.title: The "moment of arrest" is the prompt we use to invite inmates to consider their arrest in a different way- with lines, s...
items.content_text:
The "moment of arrest" is the prompt we use to invite inmates to consider their arrest in a different way- with lines, shapes, colors while focusing on good composition, contrast and complimentary colors. Because the subject matter can be highly charged emotionally, the inmates tend to do surprising work that's often aesthetically strong. They also find clues to the how and why these things happen in reliving a past in a different flavored way. 
 The scenes always involve people - so hard to draw... this time they were asked to use stars - same 5 extremities but easy and a good modification in the lesson plan. Check out the "cuffed" stars!
